data.title: Mondelez International trades higher after Q1 results top expectations
data.description:
Mondelez International (MDLZ) swung higher in postmarket trading after the food giant reported fourth-quarter results.

Organic sales were up 3.0% during the quarter to smash the consensus estimate of +0.3%, led by an 11.3% rise for the Asia, Middle East & Africa segment and a 5.1% gain for the Latin America segment. Organic sales were up 0.6% for the North American business. Overall, pricing was up 0.9% in Q1, and volume/mix was down 0.5%.

Total revenue was up 8.3% year-over-year to $10.1B to top the consensus estimate by $340M.

The Chicago-based company's adjusted gross profit margin decreased 270 basis points to 30.7% of sales, driven primarily by higher input cost inflation and unfavorable volume/mix, partially offset by higher pricing and lower manufacturing costs driven by productivity.

Adjusted EPS was down 14.9% year over year to $0.67 vs. $0.73 consensus and $0.68 a year ago. The EPS decrease was driven by operating declines and higher income taxes, partially offset by lower interest and other expenses and fewer shares outstanding.

Looking ahead, Mondelez (MDLZ) sees organic revenue growth in the range of 0% to 2% for the full year and adjusted EPS growth of 0% to 5% on a constant currency basis. The company also expects 2026 free cash flow of $3B.

"The fundamentals of our business remain strong, the capabilities of our people are unmatched, and we continue to boldly invest behind our long-term growth opportunities to enable sustained performance for years to come," summarized CEO Dirk Van de Put.

Shares of Mondelez International (MDLZ) were up 1.7% in postmarket trading after gaining 2.0% during the regular session.

data.title: Eni signs deal to restart heavy oil project in Venezuela
data.description:
Eni (E) said Tuesday it signed an ​agreement Tuesday with Venezuela's oil ministry and state-run oil company PDVSA ‌to restart the Junín-5 heavy crude oil project in the Orinoco Belt.

The Italian company said it signed the deal to resume work at the venture after a meeting between CEO Claudio Descalzi and Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez in Caracas.

"This is one of the most important bets on our country in recent times," Rodriguez said.

Junín-5, in which PDVSA holds 60% and Eni (E) owns 40%, contains ~35B barrels of oil in place and had stalled for years because of sanctions and payment disputes.

Eni (E) also recently signed a deal to sustain and increase production at the Cardón IV gas project, which includes the Perla field, described as "the largest offshore gas discovery in Latin America."

The company's production in Venezuela averaged 64K boe/day in 2025, largely from Perla, accounting for ~35% of the country’s gas consumption.

data.title: Design Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.29

• Design Therapeutics press release (DSGN): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.29.

  • Cash Position and Operating Runway: Cash, cash equivalents and investment securities were $222.8 million as of March 31, 2026, which the company expects to fund its planned operations into 2029.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Tuesday after hours: STX, SBUX, TMUS, V
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages closed lower on Tuesday after the United Arab Emirates announced its departure from OPEC, effective May 1.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) lost -0.5%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended down by -0.8%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) handed back 0.1%.

Now, here are the four stocks to watch on Tuesday after hours:

  • Seagate Technology (STX) shares rose over 10% in extended trading on Tuesday after the storage company reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's estimates by a wide margin.
  • Starbucks Corporation (SBUX) climbed 5% in postmarket action after issuing its fiscal Q2 earnings report. The Seattle-based company recorded its third straight quarter of positive comparable sales after a streak of seven quarters in negative territory. "Our second quarter marked the turn in our turnaround as our Back to Starbucks plan drove both top- and bottom-line growth," highlighted CEO Brian Niccol.
  • T-Mobile stock (TMUS) rose slightly in initial after-hours trading after posting Q1 earnings where it beat expectations on top and bottom lines and boosted full-year subscriber growth guidance.
  • Visa (V) stock jumped 4.1% in after-hours trading after the payment network's fiscal Q2 earnings handily topped Wall Street expectations, as consumers continued to spend, and the company announced a new stock buyback program.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Seagate Technology beats Q3 earnings and revenue estimates; AI demand lifts margins
Element Solutions Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.03, revenue of $840M beats by $130.54M
data.description:
• Element Solutions press release (ESI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $840M (+41.4% Y/Y) beats by $130.54M.
    • Reported net income for the first quarter of 2026 was $56 million, as compared to $98 million for the first quarter of 2025, a decrease of 43%.
      • Net income margin decreased by 980 basis points to 6.7%.
    • Adjusted EBITDA for the first quarter of 2026 was $162 million, as compared to $128 million for the first quarter of 2025. On a constant currency basis, adjusted EBITDA increased 21%.
      • Adjusted EBITDA would have been $170 million if the Micromax acquisition had closed on January 1, 2026.
      • Electronics: Adjusted EBITDA was $119 million, an increase of 34%. On a constant currency basis, adjusted EBITDA increased 29%. The Micromax business had a positive impact of 11% on constant currency adjusted EBITDA growth.
      • Specialties: Adjusted EBITDA was $43 million, an increase of 9%. On a constant currency basis, adjusted EBITDA increased 3%. The EFC Gases & Advanced Materials business had a positive impact of 9% on constant currency adjusted EBITDA growth. The MacDermid Graphics Solutions business had a negative impact of 13% on constant currency adjusted EBITDA growth.
      • Adjusted EBITDA margin increased by 170 basis points to 27.8%.

    Updated 2026 Guidance

    The Company now expects full year 2026 adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $665 million to $685 million, inclusive of expected contributions from the Micromax and EFC acquisitions on a full-year basis and assuming current exchange rates and metal prices, and adjusted EPS growth rate in the high teens. In addition, the Company expects second quarter 2026 adjusted EBITDA to be between $155 million and $170 million.

data.title: Nabors Non-GAAP EPS of -$1.54 beats by $0.49, revenue of $783.5M beats by $10.16M
data.description:
• Nabors press release (NBR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$1.54 beats by $0.49.
  • Revenue of $783.5M (-5.1% Y/Y) beats by $10.16M.

  • Consolidated adjusted free cash flow was negative $48 million in the first quarter, compared to negative $61 million in the first quarter of 2025, reflecting a $13 million improvement year-over-year. This was driven primarily by lower cash interest payments.
